title: The best way to be politically active is to sign petitions on petitions.com

transcript.text: Hey, hello. This video is what I think about the best way, what I think is the best way for the average person to be politically active.
Now, I think the average person should try to be politically active. First, I'll try to explain that one in case somebody doesn't agree with me.
And somebody might go, oh, why should I be politically active? It's no use. Well, it's very easy, actually, to be politically active.
And you can kind of kill two birds with one stone by doing it the way that I pose. You can educate yourself, which is good, and then also have your voice heard at the same time.
And this is how you do it. There's a website called thepetitionsite.com. At least I think that's what it's called.
And I think there was another one, too, another petition site. And this was years ago when I was doing this. So maybe by now there's even more.
And then you have other, like, community-type sites like MySpace that are politically oriented where you can join a cause and stuff.
And I don't really exactly know how that goes, but I think that's a good way to go, too.
But the best way, I think, for the average person to be politically active is to join the petition site.
And I was doing this for a while, and I'm going to try and do it again.
I'm silly and stupid me. I haven't kept up.
But what you do is you find a bunch of causes that you believe in or, you know, things you believe in,
And then you click the button and you sign up for them, and then they send you emails about their cause or other cause related to it.
And then you can read the little blip, and then you sign your name.
But you don't just sign your name. They let you write a little blip, a little blurb.
So it's cool. You get to write your little blurb, put your two cents in, and then the person who creates this petition
Has a goal of signatures that they think that they need in order to get done what they think they need to get done.
And then once you get to that amount of signatures, you can print up your thing or put it on a disk or whatever,
And then send it to the congressman or whoever you need to send it to.
And then, you know, as we live in a democracy, you know that our voices will be heard.
They'll record how many people signed this petition or did this or that or the next thing.
And so, for example, if there's one about what you want to do, maybe legalize marijuana or something,
You can get a bunch of people to sign that and then turn it in.
And then if it doesn't happen, then, you know, maybe you can take it from there.
But, you know, alert the media that, you know, I had 500,000 signatures,
And yet it wasn't, and still it didn't pass, and why?
Yeah, you know, the numbers don't coincide.
It's much easier to prove that the populace is for or against a certain thing.
And so it's a way to get the government to respond,
Because I guess the government doesn't always respond like that.
You know, sometimes people will have a good idea of a way to spend money
Or a way to do this or that or the next thing,
And they turn it in, but they don't, you know, they don't have any money,
They don't have any backing by some group or, you know,
They're not a powerful person or whatever who knows people or whatever,
So it doesn't pass.
But, you know, you don't have to, you know, one way people right now,
What we're used to about getting something done,
If you want to get something done, you create a group of people,
A coalition of people that, but that's kind of a waste of time,
Because in that coalition it turns into a bureaucracy.
You have to have officers and you've got to pay your dues
And then you've got to go to boring meetings.
And it's like it's much more, much less time-consuming and funner
Because there's more input per individual to just do the petition route
Where you just sign your name on the petition, write your little thing,
And then there you go.
You've made your voice heard.
You never had to go to a meeting, never had to pay a due
If you do a coalition or whatever, and it works out great.
And at the same time, at the beginning of the petition,
You get to read what they're talking about.
And, you know, anybody can turn in a petition too.
So if you get excited about signing petitions,
Well, why not make your own petition?
And, you know, it doesn't have to be limited to certain political issues
And ways to spend money, you know.
Money is limited.
It can also be random stuff, you know.
Like, I want people to sign the petition where it's cool to have a facial tattoo or something,
You know, if you want to make people more tolerant generally
And it doesn't have to do with the law.
You just want people to be more tolerant.
And then you go, see, I have the petition, 50,000 signatures.
This means that you have to respect people with tattoos
And you can put it on your website or whatever.
But mainly the petition should be about political issues and stuff.
What I remember, and I haven't seen the site for years,
But what I remember about most of the petitions people sign is,
Is, like, I think, oh, I can't,
I'm thinking maybe, like, spend money on this or something.
But personally, I'm not so into it.
I mean, I'd sign the petition and I'd write just to support the cause,
But I wouldn't make a petition to, like, you know,
Make a new bill to spend money on this or whatever
Because that just makes bureaucracy more expensive
When you keep throwing money on a new thing.
If I were to make a petition,
I would make a petition to stop spending money on something that's not needed
Because I think there's lots of programs and stuff that are not needed,
And I don't know what they are, but I'll have to look, you know,
I'll look in them, I'm sure I'll find them.
Some of them, for example, in my opinion, the FCC,
The DEA,
And the FDA as far as testing natural stuff.
You know, those guys should be testing chemicals,
Not natural stuff that doesn't pollute the environment
And make somebody as hella rich
And keep people from being able to use natural stuff.
So, I'm going to get right on the petition site.
This is kind of like my mission statement,
That it's time for me to get on that petition site and sign petitions, you know,
So that when I open up my email every day,
I can have like ten petitions
That I get to sign, I get to click on,
And that can be like my way of watching the news, you know.
I go, oh, cool, look at that.
Okay, I'll sign it and I'll write my thing.
And so that's what I think is the ultimate form of democracy.
It's similar to multinational corporations,
Which I think is a good example of like kind of the ultimate democracy.
It's people throwing in their money for a cause
That will also make them money.
You know, also, unfortunately, these days,
People only care about money,

Whether or not they think it's good,
Just because they think it will make money, you know.
Like somebody investing in Coca-Cola,
Just because they think Coca-Cola is going to make

Because they opened up a new plant or something like that,
